name: CapoDappProvider
activities: Provides React context wrapping Capo instance, wallet connection, and network client
Initializes TxBatcher and batch submission infrastructure
Manages wallet connect/disconnect lifecycle
Polls for Capo instance changes

name: CapoDappProviderContext
activities: Defines React Context for provider access
Provides useCapoDappProvider hook with typed Capo access
Tracks mount state and generates component IDs

name: Batch Submission UI
activities: Displays transaction count and aggregate batch status
Provides master/detail view of transactions
Shows review panel for pre-sign inspection
Manages detail panel visibility lifecycle
Subscribes to BatchSubmitController events for live updates

name: CharterStatus Dashboard
activities: Loads and displays charter data
Detects needed upgrades
Renders manifest entries with delegate health
Flags delegates needing script updates

name: FormManager
activities: Binds to HTML form elements via callback ref
Tracks field changes for delegated data records
Provides field error validation feedback

name: TxBatcher
activities: Manages batch lifecycle with active and previous slots
Implements batch rotation on terminal state
Guards rotation behind canRotate check
Emits rotated event for UI subscription

name: RateMeterGauge
activities: Renders circular SVG gauge for rate-limiter metrics
Subscribes to CachedUtxoIndex event emitter

name: BatchSubmitController
activities: Manages per-batch state machine
Tracks registered and built transactions
Provides aggregate state summaries
Emits change events for UI consumption

name: TxSubmitMgr
activities: Manages single-transaction submission to single submitter
Implements retry with exponential backoff
Detects expiry, unknown UTxO, and fork errors

name: TxSubmissionTracker
activities: Tracks per-transaction state across multiple submitters
Coordinates TxSubmitMgr instances

name: Batch Review & Sign
steps: Application builds transactions via mkTxn methods
BatchSubmitController queues transactions
UI detects txAdded, shows badge
User expands detail panel for review
User initiates signing
Wallet prompts for batch signature
Controller transitions through submitting/confirming
UI collapses to pending indicator
Controller reaches confirmed, UI shows checkmark

name: Batch Rotation
steps: BatchSubmitController reaches terminal state
User or application triggers new transaction
TxBatcher checks canRotate
TxBatcher rotates: current becomes previous, new batch created
UI receives rotated event, updates display
Previous batch shown as lightweight indicator
New batch accepts queued transactions

name: Data Forms Transform
steps: Bridge readData produces strict type T from UPLC
Form transform converts strict type fields to HTML primitives (strings, numbers)
User edits form values
Form transform converts HTML values to permissive type TLike
Bridge mkData converts permissive type to UPLC

name: Data Lists Transform
steps: Bridge readData produces strict type T from UPLC
List transform converts to display-friendly representation
UI renders formatted data in table or list
